science.title: Scientists Analyzed First Results from Blue Ghost Lunar Lander and Made a Discovery No One Expected

science.title: Astronomers found most pristine star ever
science.description: Astronomers discover the most pristine star ever found, offering new clues about the first stars, early supernovae, and the origins of elements in the universe.

science.title: Most U.S. states are warming but not in the way you think
science.description: Warming across the U.S. is far more uneven than it looks at first glance. While only about half of states show rising average temperatures, most are heating up in specific ways—like hotter highs or warmer lows. These hidden shifts vary by region, with the West seeing more extreme heat and the North losing cold extremes. The findings suggest climate change is playing out differently depending on where you live.

science.title: Zebrafish gon4la mutants recapitulate human GON4L-related growth disorders and reveal novel metabolic organs abnormalities
science.description: Biallelic mutations in human GON4L have been linked to short stature, craniofacial abnormalities, and microcephaly, yet the underlying mechanisms remain unclear. GON4L is a nuclear protein composed of a YY1AP1-like region, two PAH domains, and one SANT domain, all implicated in transcriptional or chromatin regulation. Here, we define the function of Gon4la (a zebrafish ortholog of human GON4L) in post-embryonic growth and tissue homeostasis. We generated three gon4la mutant lines using CRISPR-Cas9, each predicted to express truncated proteins lacking the C-terminal PAH/SANT domains and differing in N-terminal YY1AP1-like region retention. All gon4la mutants displayed proportionate dwarfism with intestinal and pancreatic abnormalities, accompanied by expanded progenitor proliferation and impaired epithelial differentiation. Only mutants completely lacking the N-terminal YY1AP1-like region exhibited reduced igf1a expression, elevated growth hormone pathway activity, and increased mortality, consistent with endocrine dysregulation. Our findings demonstrate that distinct domains of Gon4la contribute differentially to systemic growth and metabolic organ homeostasis. Zebrafish gon4la mutants provide a tractable vertebrate model for dissecting mechanisms of GON4L-related developmental disorder, Li-Takada-Miyake syndrome (LTMS), and for functional testing of rare GON4L variants.

science.title: NASA's water-hunting tool will help scout moon's South Pole
science.description: NASA is joining international partners to hunt for ice on the moon in support of future human exploration. The agency is providing a water-detecting instrument, the Neutron Spectrometer System (NSS), to the Lunar Polar Exploration (LUPEX) mission led by JAXA (Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency) and ISRO (Indian Space Research Organization).

science.title: Physicists use pulses of light in 37 dimensions to prove quantum paradox
science.description: Physicists built a 37-dimensional optical test of a three-context GHZ paradox, sharpening evidence for quantum contextuality.

science.title: Artemis II crew move closer to moon than Earth on historic mission
science.description: Nasa crew members can now see the moon, which one described as ‘a beautiful sight’

science.title: These overlooked brain cells may control fear and PTSD
science.description: Astrocytes, once thought to be mere brain “support cells,” are now revealed to be key players in fear memory. Researchers found they actively help form, recall, and weaken fear responses by interacting with neurons in real time. Changing astrocyte activity directly altered how strong fear memories became. This breakthrough could lead to entirely new treatments for anxiety-related disorders.
